ClockNodes.Name: PWR SEQ
ClockNodes.Signpost: The Power Sequencer is always enabled.

ClockNodes.Name: RTC
ClockNodes.Signpost: The RTC is a 32-bit binary timer that keeps the time of day up to 136 years.

ClockNodes.Name: SYSCLK_DIV_2
ClockNodes.Signpost: This divider has a fixed divisor of 2 and cannot be configured.

ClockNodes.Name: INRO
ClockNodes.Signpost: To use this oscillator, please select the INRO in one of the clock source multiplexers.

ClockNodes.Name: HFIO
ClockNodes.Signpost: To use this oscillator, please select the HFIO in one of the clock source multiplexers.

ClockNodes.Name: 32KIN
ClockNodes.Signpost: The 32KIN pin should be connected to an external clock source and cannot be configured.

ClockNodes.Name: 32KOUT
ClockNodes.Signpost: The 32KOUT pin receives the output from the ERTCO and cannot be configured.

ClockNodes.Name: Cortex-M4
ClockNodes.Signpost: To set up the clock for the Cortex-M4, please configure the multiplexers and dividers on the canvas.

ClockNodes.Name: ERTCO
ClockNodes.Signpost: Use of the ERTCO may be controlled by configuring the ERTCO Bypass multiplexer on the canvas.

ClockNodes.Name: 32KCAL
ClockNodes.Signpost: To configure the calibration output on P0.2, the OSC_32KCAL signal must be assigned using the Pin Config tool.

ClockNodes.Name: SPIMSS
ClockNodes.Signpost: The SPIMSS peripheral provides independent serial communication support for I2S (Inter-IC Sound) for 16-bit mono or stereo audio transfer to or from an external I2S audio codec. The SPIMSS can also be configured as either a SPI controller (in single or multi-controller systems) or a SPI target.

Registers.Documentation: Each bit controls the GPIO_EN setting for one GPIO pin on the associated port. These bits configure the corresponding device pin for digital I/O or an alternate function mode. The fields can be modified directly by writing to this register or indirectly through GPIOn_EN0_SET or GPIOn_EN0_CLR.

Fields.Name: PWM
Fields.Documentation: Timer PWM Match: in PWM Mode, This Field Sets the Count Value for the First Transition Period of the PWM Cycle. at the End of the Cycle Where CNT Equals PWM, the PWM Output Transitions to the Second Period of the PWM Cycle. the Second PWM Period Count is Stored in the CMP Register. the Value Set for PWM Must Me Less Than the Value Set in CMP for PWM Mode Operation. Timer Capture Value: in Capture, Compare, and Capture/Compare Modes, This Field is Used to Store the CNT Value When a Capture, Compare, or Capture/Compare Event Occurs.

Registers.Name: TMR2_CMP
Registers.Documentation: This register stores the compare value, which is used to set the maximum count value to initiate a reload of the timer to 0x0001.
